design_philosophy.core_loop: Non-linear mansion exploration with room-clearing combat, map-based navigation, and time pressure creating emergent narrative outcomes
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure vs. exploration depth: players must balance thorough level investigation against clock-based mission objectives that alter story outcomes
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Brutal visceral power fantasy as a masked monster-hunter protecting family; escalation from human fighter to grotesque mutant form via resource accumulation
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Unique approach to difficulty and narrative coupling—time limits are not hard failure states but narrative consequence engines. Same level completion can yield radically different story branches (spouse alive/dead/transformed, child saved or lost). Rejects traditional linear progression in favor of mansion-as-playground with agency-driven outcomes. Celebrates graphic violence as over-the-top spectacle rather than realism.

difficulty.lives_system: Extra lives scattered throughout levels; health items available for collection
difficulty.continues_system: Passwords allow return to specific levels, enabling non-linear retry structure
difficulty.checkpointing: Room-based progression with map visibility; players can choose routes after clearing enemy encounters
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High—time limits do not cause game over but instead trigger narrative branching. Players can prioritize speed (save family) or thorough exploration (resource gathering for power-ups). Power Meter transformation mechanic provides escalating power scaling tied to Eldritch Orb collection, allowing self-directed difficulty modulation.

progression.upgrade_system: Power Meter system: collecting Eldritch Orbs (found in levels or dropped by enemies) fills transformation meter, converting Rick into muscular mutant form with enhanced damage output and special flesh-weapon attack. Weapon pickups (baseball bat, cleaver, etc.) provide temporary stat boosts but are flung rather than wielded by transformed Rick.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Level completion gates narrative progression; time performance determines which family members remain alive, creating four distinct ending states. Non-linear room navigation within levels encourages backtracking and secret discovery.
replayability_hooks: Four distinct endings based on time management and rescue priority (both family alive, one dies, both die)
Multiple routes through mansion rooms after combat encounters create varied spatial navigation paths
Password system enables targeted level returns to explore alternative time strategies
Power-up location discovery rewards thorough exploration over speed-running

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling through mansion layout and room design reflecting domestic invasion
Time-gated narrative consequences: failure to save family members mid-level results in permanent story alterations (Jennifer devoured by boreworm, David kidnapped by Evil One)
Mask dialogue reveals plot escalation and thematic commentary on evil's persistence
Ending cinematics dynamically change tone based on survival outcomes: melancholic/defeated vs. triumphant depending on family rescue success

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters serve as narrative gates rather than pure skill tests. Boreworm encounter tied to Jennifer rescue mission creates time pressure; Evil One final boss encounter gates David rescue and mask destruction. Bosses are mechanically straightforward but narratively weighted with emotional stakes.
notable_bosses: Boreworm (Level 2): Jennifer rescue gate; time limit forces speed over caution
Evil One: Final antagonist; reveals mask's deception and forces climactic destruction sequence

faction_indicators: Family (Rick/Jennifer/David) vs. Mask (corrupting voice encouraging violence)
Wall Street success identity vs. Horror protagonist identity—mansion represents attempted domesticity invaded by supernatural forces
danger_signals: Eldritch Orb collection as corruption mechanism—power accumulation visually/mechanically transforms player character into monster
Mask's continued existence conditional on human suffering (as stated in bad endings), suggesting cycle of violence perpetuation
Time pressure narrative mechanic creates stakes where inaction = family death, encoding urgency as moral imperative
environmental_storytelling: Connecticut mansion setting grounds supernatural horror in domestic Americana context
Room-based exploration reveals family possessions and layout, humanizing stakes before tragedy strikes
Weapon pickups (everyday objects: baseball bat, cleaver) contrast mundane domesticity with grotesque combat purpose
